items.title: 54-year-old man nabbed for exposing himself to two girls
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor police have arrested a 54-year-old man for allegedly exposing himself to two underage girls while seated in his car in Bandar Baru Uda here yesterday. In the 1pm incident, a closed-circuit television recording installed at a house in the housing area captured a black car idling along Jalan Padi Mahsuri 10 as two girls walked past. Seconds later, the driver called out to the girls.

items.title: Maxim e-hailing operating legally nationwide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Maxim e-hailing platform is now operating legally nationwide after receiving official approval from the relevant authorities. Maxim Malaysia director Mohd Hazwan Musley said the company has obtained a valid licence and fully complied with the regulatory requirements stipulated by the Ministry of Transport (MOT), the Road Transport Department (JPJ), the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD) and the Commercial Vehicle Licensing Board (CVLB). He said that on Dec 2, 2025, APAD published an official statement on Facebook listing all registered e-hailing companies operating in Peninsular Malaysia, and Maxim was also listed as having a valid permit.

items.title: Myanmar refugee's death ruled homicide: US health officials
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Health officials on Wednesday ruled the death of a Rohingya refugee from Myanmar who was found dead on a street in New York state after his release from immigration detention a homicide. The manner of death – how the cause came about – was homicide, it said. Shah Alam's next of kin were notified about the findings, the county health department said.

items.title: Economy minister: Malaysian Vision Valley Project 60pc complete, targeted to finish this year despite delays
items.summary: NILAI, April 2 — The Malaysian Vision Valley (MVV) project is showing renewed progress after previous delays and is now targeted for completion within the year, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. After a site visit here today, the minister confirmed that the project, a massive state-led economic growth corridor in Negeri Sembilan, has surpassed 60 per cent completion following the appointment of a rescue contractor. “Taking into account the challenges involved, the target remains to complete the project within this year, with emphasis placed on expediting the works,” he saiid, adding that the project’s cost remains within the existing contract terms.

items.title: We can’t pass on rising costs: Malaysia’s tourism industry pleads for urgent govt aid, subsidies
items.summary: The Malaysian Tourism Federation (MTF) said the Malaysian government needs to urgently help the local tourism industry now, as the tourism operators’ financial burden continues to increase. MTF president Sri Ganesh Michiel explained how Malaysia’s travel industry works, where tour package prices are locked in advance: Malaysia’s travel agencies design travel packages and then negotiate and sell them to travel agencies abroad. Sri Ganesh Michiel, president of Malaysian Tourism Federation, should immediately give out fuel subsidies to tourism transport operators, as the local tourism industry may otherwise be forced to absorb higher costs and bear losses.

items.title: Indonesia calls Israel death penalty law 'grave violation' of human rights
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia has criticised Israel's approval of a death penalty bill applicable to Palestinians in the occupied West Bank as a "grave violation of international human rights and humanitarian law." Under the law, passed in parliament on Monday, Palestinians in the occupied West Bank convicted by military courts of carrying out deadly attacks classified as "terrorism" will face the death penalty as a default sentence. "Indonesia also calls on the international community, in particular the United Nations, to take firm measures to ensure accountability and protection for the Palestinian people," Jakarta said.

items.title: 'Results proof syllabus is effective'
items.summary: SIJIL Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) candidates have bounced back from years of Covid-19 pandemic disruption, achieving the best national average grade (GPK) since the introduction of Kurikulum Standard Sekolah Menengah (KSSM) in 2021. "SPM 2025 candidates have undergone a long and continuous teaching and learning process. The GPK for urban candidates improved to 4.4 from 4.47 in 2024, whereas for rural candidates, it improved to 4.81 from 4.92 previously.

items.title: Chief Justice: AI is a tool, not a replacement for human judgment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The legal profession must guard against becoming overly dependent on artificial intelligence (AI), Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh said, cautioning that technology should not erode the human element in the practice of law. "Technology makes things faster, but I dare say it can make us less human. Present were Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, and Universiti Malaya pro-chancellor Tan Sri Datuk Zainun Ali.

items.title: Digital resilience a national security priority
items.summary: National security increasingly depends on keeping essential digital services online and maintaining public trust in systems people rely on every day. Telecommunications networks are no longer just commercial infrastructure. Governments increasingly recognise telecommunication networks as National Critical Information Infrastructure (NCII), alongside energy and water systems.

items.title: Cops: Drunk driving rooted in attitude, not just enforcement
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Alcohol consumption is not merely a matter of personal choice but carries moral responsibility and implications for public safety, police said. Bukit Aman Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department (TEID) director Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said the issue of drunk driving must be addressed at its root through stronger values and discipline among road users. "The main challenge is not just enforcement on the ground, but shaping awareness, responsibility and moral values among road users.

items.title: Nearly 900 cases recorded during Hari Raya price control scheme
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A total of 896 cases were recorded during the Festive Season Maximum Price Scheme (SHMMP) for Hari Raya Aidilfitri 2026, involving compounds totaling RM99,800 and seizures valued at RM32,015.70. Pink price tags are enforced by the ministry to identify controlled-price items during festive seasons. Only two cases involved failing to display any price tags, while another two cases were recorded for selling items above the maximum price.

items.title: MCMC probes multiple cases of fake news on fuel, electricity price hikes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) is investigating eight individuals suspected of spreading false information on alleged increases in fuel and electricity prices via social media platforms. In a statement, MCMC said five individuals are being investigated over misleading claims of a hike in petrol and diesel prices circulated on Threads, while three others are linked to false content on electricity tariff increases shared on Facebook, Threads and WhatsApp. Initial checks found that the content had been manipulated using graphic elements resembling those of established media organisations.

items.title: Penang KPDN seizes RM415,877 in subsidised diesel, steps up crackdown on misuse
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, April 2 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) in Penang has recorded eight cases of misappropriated subsidised diesel, with total seizures amounting to RM415,877.95. Penang KPDN director S. Jegan said the cases, recorded between January and April this year, resulted in five arrests. KPDN Penang also received four public complaints related to the misuse of subsidised fuel.

items.title: SP Setia launches Hari Raya sales campaign
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: SP Setia Bhd has rolled out a Hari Raya Aidilfitri sales campaign featuring buyer rewards, referral incentives and festive activities aimed at boosting homeownership and community engagement. The campaign, running from March 16 to April 30, 2026, targets new homebuyers, existing purchasers under its "Citizen Setia" programme, and the wider public. Commenting on the campaign, SP Setia president and chief executive officer Datuk Zaini Yusoff said the Aidilfitri campaign underscores the group's focus on making quality homeownership more attainable.

items.title: Azam stays tight-lipped on Bloomberg report: ‘I don’t want to comment’
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 2 — Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki today declined to comment on recent reports by Bloomberg concerning allegations linked to his shareholdings. Bloomberg previously published an investigative report highlighting Azam’s shareholdings in several listed companies, alongside allegations that senior MACC officers had colluded with a group of businessmen in attempts to take control of targeted firms. Azam and the MACC have denied any wrongdoing.

items.title: [UPDATED] DoE to see new director-general after top 2 officers face court charges
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A new Department of Environment (DoE) director-general is expected to be appointed after the department's two most senior officers are charged in court tomorrow. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the matter would be left to the Chief Secretary to the Government, but he expects a new appointment once the charges are filed. Last month, the MACC submitted investigation papers recommending charges against two senior DoE officials over alleged corruption linked to e-waste and plastic waste management.

items.title: Two senior DoE officials to be charged tomorrow
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Department of Environment (DoE) director-general and deputy director-general are expected to be charged in court tomorrow. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the Attorney General's Chambers has granted approval for prosecution. Azam said the case would likely be heard at the Sessions Court in Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: MACC chief urges public to see beyond ‘bad apples’ in law enforcement agencies
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 2 — Public perception towards enforcement officers must change, Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said today, noting that the actions of a few “bad apples” often overshadow the majority who serve with integrity. Speaking at the Multiagency Taskforce Engagement Session at MACC headquarters here, Azam said negative perceptions frequently arise whenever issues of corruption or misconduct surface, even though many officers continue to carry out their duties professionally. “Enforcement personnel are frequently associated with corruption whenever any issue arises.

items.title: MACC chief: Corporate criminals manipulating shares openly, agencies must keep up
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 2 — Share manipulation and other forms of corporate crime are increasingly being carried out openly, Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said today, stressing the need for authorities to step up efforts to detect such activities. Speaking at the Multiagency Taskforce Engagement Session at the MACC headquarters here, Azam said corporate criminals are using complex structures and professional networks to hide fraudulent activities, including tax evasion, money laundering and manipulation of shareholdings. He said corporate criminals also often rely on networks of professionals such as accountants and lawyers, as well as insiders within relevant agencies, to facilitate illegal activities while reducing the risk of detection.

items.title: Major earthquake in Indonesia kills one, sparks panic
items.summary: MANADO, Indonesia: A major 7.4-magnitude quake struck off the coast of eastern Indonesia on Thursday, killing one person, damaging buildings and sending people running into the streets in panic. The early-morning tremor in the Molucca Sea between the Sulawesi and Maluku island groups triggered waves up to 75 centimetres (2.5 feet) high in places and prompted a tsunami warning that was subsequently lifted. The Hawaii-based Pacific Tsunami Warning Centre (PTWC) initially said hazardous tsunami waves were possible within 1,000 kilometres (621 miles) of the epicentre along the coasts of Indonesia, the Philippines and Malaysia.

items.title: Johor Customs bust illegal ketum water factory, seize RM76,796 in leaves and bottled liquid
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 2 — The Johor Customs Department has recently raided an illegal ketum water processing factory in Taman Kempas Utama here with a seizure of RM76,7963 worth of bottles. The house raid netted 103 kilogrammes of ketum leaves and 189.50 litres of ketum water with a total value of RM9,805. Following that, a total of 442.9 kilogrammes of ketum leaves and 1,642.5 litres of ketum water with a total value of RM66,991 was later seized at the factory.

items.title: [UPDATED] MATF records 3,523 arrests, RM6.87bil in seizures from 2023 to 2025
items.summary: "Within this period, 3,523 arrests were made, involving major operations such as Ops Samba 2.0, Ops Taufan, Ops Yellow, Ops Counter Setting and Ops Bunga. More importantly, the success in dismantling large-scale organised syndicates through operations like Ops White, Ops Kingfruit, Ops X, Ops Sikaro, Ops Metal, Ops Karen and Ops Grip shows that MATF's approach is capable of penetrating complex and structured criminal networks across multiple sectors," he said during a special engagement session today. He added that as MATF's lead agency, MACC also spearheaded several high-impact operations, including Operasi 82S, Ops Tropicana, Ops Eagle and Ops Liter.

items.title: Azam declines to comment on contract extension
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has declined to comment on matters relating to his contract extension. When approached on the sidelines of a special engagement session at the MACC headquarters today, Azam reiterated that he would not address questions concerning the issue. "No comment.

items.title: Anwar admits justice still out of reach for many, vows reforms to close gap
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim acknowledged today that many of Malaysia’s poorest and most marginalised still lack meaningful access to justice, a gap his government aims to bridge. He said that, for this reason, his government has made access to justice a central pillar of its broader reform agenda. He said it bears remembering that the law facilitates the day-to-day lives of citizens, reminding current and upcoming law practitioners to ensure the law is utilised for the public good.

items.title: Economic strain could drive rise in corruption, warns Azam Baki
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Economic pressures could lead to a rise in corruption and financial crimes if governance is not strengthened, Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has warned. He said periods of economic strain tend to heighten financial stress among the public, creating conditions that may drive an increase in illicit activities. "When economic difficulties occur, financial pressures tied to survival increase.

items.title: MMA: GP fee revision crucial step to strengthen primary care
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Medical Association (MMA) has welcomed the gazettement of new regulations revising general practitioner (GP) consultation fees, describing it as a long-overdue move to strengthen primary care. MMA said GP consultation fees had remained unchanged since 2006, based on a structure dating back to 1992, and were no longer sustainable under current healthcare demands. MMA said the revised fee structure introduces flexibility, allowing consultation charges to better reflect clinical complexity, market conditions and patient needs.

items.title: Takeaways from Trump's speech on Iran
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump staunchly defended his handling of the month-old US-Israeli war on Iran in a prime-time address on Wednesday, saying the US military was nearing completion of its mission while also reinforcing his threats to bomb the Islamic republic back to the Stone Age. And he suggested the war could escalate if Iranian leaders did not capitulate to US terms during negotiations, with strikes on Iran's energy and oil infrastructure possible. The Strait of Hormuz Trump's comments on Wednesday were not clear about whether US military operations could end even before Iran reopened the Strait of Hormuz, the vital waterway on which it has a chokehold that has created the worst global energy shock in history.

items.title: What's real anymore? AI warps truth of Middle East war
items.summary: WASHINGTON, UNITED STATES: "Is Netanyahu real or AI?" "Last time I checked, humans usually don't have 6 fingers... AI does," said one post on X, garnering nearly five million views. "More AI," said one viral Threads post, questioning why his cup remained full after a large sip.

items.title: MACC completes probe into White & Case over 1MDB-linked funds, recommends charges
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has completed its investigation into international law firm White & Case over its alleged links to misappropriated 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) funds, with a recommendation for charges to be filed against several individuals. He said investigations also examined payments made to the firm believed to involve funds directly linked to the misappropriation of 1MDB assets. The investigation follows earlier disclosures that MACC had intensified its probe into an international law firm linked to financial transactions involving 1MDB funds.

items.title: UMW Toyota unveils new battery EV line-up, strengthens push toward low-carbon mobility
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: UMW Toyota Motor (UMWT) has unveiled its latest battery electric vehicle (BEV) lineup, marking a major step in supporting Malaysia's transition towards low-carbon mobility, in line with the National Energy Transition Roadmap. Sim said the shift towards electric mobility would reduce reliance on fossil fuels and create better jobs, strengthen innovation and position Malaysia as a competitive player in evolving the global automotive landscape. "This is why I am excited to witness Toyota's new BEV to add to the total industry volume, reflecting how Malaysia is moving beyond early adoptions toward a more sustainable phase of growth in electrified mobility," he said.

items.title: Police raise alarm as drink-driving continues to turn deadly
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police recorded 27 fatal accidents involving intoxicated drivers in 2024, compared with 31 cases the previous year, underscoring persistent concerns over drink-driving despite relatively low overall figures. Bukit Aman Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department (TEID) director Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said each case carries severe consequences, particularly the loss of life, and must not be taken lightly. Hasbullah said the increased deployment is aimed not only at detecting offenders but also at creating a strong deterrent effect among road users.

items.title: MAG extends profit streak as revenue climbs to RM14.5bil in 2025
items.summary: The parent company of Malaysia Airlines Bhd reported a six per cent year-on-year increase in total revenue to RM14.5 billion for the financial year ended 2025 compared with the previous year. However, MAG ended 2025 with a lower cash balance of RM1.53 billion compared to RM3 billion in 2024. The RM3.6 billion was pumped into the group by its sole shareholder, Khazanah Nasional Bhd following MAG's financial restructuring.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar warns of 'law of the jungle' undermining global order [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has warned that the rule of law is increasingly being undermined in international affairs, with justice often overshadowed by power and selective enforcement. He said the rule of law must remain universally accessible and enforceable, stressing that its integrity is central to a just and orderly international system. Also present were Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Minister Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, Chief Justice Datuk Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh and Universiti Malaya Pro-Chancellor Tan Sri Datuk Zainun Ali.

items.title: Sime Darby Property award to IJM a timely boost for data centre progress amid market volatility
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Property Bhd's contract award to IJM Corp Bhd comes at an opportune time and is expected to reassure investors that construction of the two data centre facilities in Elmina Business Park is progressing as planned. Data Centre 1 and Data Centre 2 have lease values of RM2 billion and RM5.6 billion for the initial 20 years. RHB Research noted that the first income contribution from Data Centre 1 is expected to boost Sime Darby Property's full-year earnings for the financial year 2027.

items.title: Health Ministry shortens wait for medical graduate placements to two months
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry (MOH) has reduced the waiting period for medical graduate placements to two months from an average of 10 months. MOH said the improvement was achieved through measures to streamline the placement process. "MOH remains committed to ensuring that medical graduates are placed within a reasonable timeframe so they are not left without job opportunities," it said.

items.title: RHB sees margin upside in IJM Selangor data centre win
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: RHB Research expects IJM Corp Bhd's data centre construction contract in Selangor to generate a profit-before-tax margin of between five and eight per cent. The research house said that, with this latest win, IJM has secured data centre projects amounting to RM5 billion on an effective share basis. RHB Research estimates that this latest contract lifts IJM Corp's outstanding order book to RM8.2 billion.

items.title: MARKET PULSE AM APRIL 2, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia rose on Thursday, following Wall Street gains after US president Donald Trump hinted that the Middle East conflict could be nearing an end. An analyst said the key index may extend its rally for a third day, driven by hopes of easing tensions in Iran. However, market sentiment remains fragile as investors weigh geopolitical risks, high energy prices, and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: HLIB Research: Middle East tensions to lift near-term palm oil prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian palm oil prices are poised for near-term gains as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continue to influence energy markets, analysts said. Hong Leong Investment Bank (HLIB Research) highlighted that the ongoing Iran conflict has created a multi-channel shock for the plantation sector, affecting energy, fertiliser and logistics, which in turn supports crude palm oil (CPO) prices in the near term. "Elevated crude oil prices will strengthen biodiesel economics, lifting demand for vegetable oils and reinforcing CPO's role as a proxy for energy markets," it said in a note.

items.title: First South Korean in Malaysia to ace SPM: Kim Na Eun scores 10 A+ including BM
items.summary: MIRI, April 2 — Kim Na Eun, 17, has become the first South Korean student in Malaysia to score A+ in all 10 subjects she sat for in the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) 2025, including Bahasa Malaysia. We did not expect her to get straight A+ in all subjects, including Bahasa Malaysia, as she struggled with learning Bahasa Malaysia during her primary school years,” he said. Na Eun took Bahasa Melayu, English, Moral, History, Mathematics, Additional Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and Bible Knowledge for SPM 2025.

items.title: Nationwide sting: KPDN cracks down on RON95 smugglers in Johor, modified 4WD in Sabah, LPG stockpile in Perak
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 2 — The Johor Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) will intensify enforcement operations to curb cases of smuggling or misappropriation of subsidised RON95 petrol, including undercover operations, especially at 31 petrol stations near the state border. “Monitoring will be enhanced where teams will operate statically and undercover methods, especially at high-risk petrol stations,” she told Bernama. She said this when asked to comment on whether monitoring and enforcement would be intensified to curb cases of smuggling of subsidised RON95 fuel.

items.title: Peak romance: Why Syed Saddiq’s proposal left Bella Astillah breathless at 4,000 metres
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — An exhausting climb up Mount Kinabalu in January culminated in a surprise proposal for actress Bella Astillah, who has now opened up about the emotional moment she accepted Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman’s hand in marriage. Alhamdulillah, I said ‘yes’ within just a few seconds.” She also shared that her two children reacted positively to the news, a conversation initiated by Syed Saddiq himself. “My children responded very positively.

items.title: Police tracking down suspect after anti‑drugs officer injured in knife attack during Bangsar operation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Police are tracking down a local man who acted aggressively and injured an officer of the National Anti-Drugs Agency (AADK) with a knife during an operation at Jalan Kerinchi, Bangsar here yesterday. Brickfields district police chief ACP Hoo Chang Hook said the incident occurred at 5.08pm at a car wash premises next to Bangsar South Mall when the 48-year-old AADK officer suffered injuries to his palm and right thumb after being attacked by the suspect. Members of the public with information are urged to contact investigating officer Insp Syed Hamiz Azfar Syed Mohd Najib at the Brickfields district police headquarters or any nearby police station to assist in the investigation.

items.title: Fake news, real trouble: MCMC tracks down five users behind TV3-style fuel price hoax
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has launched an investigation into five individuals suspected of spreading false information on the social media platform Threads about an impending fuel price hike. The viral content, which falsely claimed that petrol and diesel prices would increase, was deemed misleading and capable of causing public anxiety. MCMC noted that the misinformation had been edited to mimic the visual identity of television broadcaster Buletin TV3.

items.title: Teenager drowns at Gunung Ledang Waterfall after slipping during outing with friends
items.summary: Tangkak Fire and Rescue Station operations commander Fazlan Maraman said the victim, Muhammad Zaffran Abu Samah, 15, was pronounced dead by the Ministry of Health staff who arrived at the scene. “Upon arrival, the Fire and Rescue Operations Team (PKO) found that the victim had fallen and drowned while bathing with two friends. He said they received an emergency call regarding the incident at 5.27 pm and a Fire Rescue Tender (FRT) together with an Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) unit, comprising eight personnel from the Tangkak Fire and Rescue Station, were dispatched to the scene.

items.title: Anthony Loke visits grieving widow, offers comfort and support after tragic crash in Klang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke paid a visit to the wife of the motorcyclist who died after being hit by a driver believed to be under the influence of alcohol and drugs in Klang, Selangor, on Sunday. He said he visited the family of the late Amirul Hafiz Omar, 33, yesterday, and was accompanied by Road Transport Department (JPJ) director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli. Loke said he also personally provided financial assistance to the man’s widow, Nor Nadia Abdul Majid, 32, to help lighten the family’s burden.

items.title: Unlicensed, unregistered, unchecked: The Selangor children’s home where boys were allegedly abused for 10 years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Police have arrested the operator of an unlicensed children’s home and three others, uncovering a horrifying decade of alleged sexual abuse that has left multiple young male residents hospitalised with injuries consistent with repeated assault. The home had been operating for approximately 10 years, the New Straits Times reported. Police have arrested four suspects, including the 31-year-old male operator and a 17-year-old resident of the home.

items.title: Johor immigration swoops on luxury apartment, detains Chinese nationals with fake Mexican passport
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 2 — The Johor Immigration Department detained four Chinese nationals for using fake passports and overstaying in the country. Its director, Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus, said the suspects, aged 43 to 57, were detained at a luxury apartment in Bayu Puteri Tuesday. “One of them used a Mexican international passport to enter and stay in the country, while the others used passports from their country of origin,” he said in a statement.

items.title: ‘I have to be both mother and father now’: Widow of Klang drink-driving victim hopes to rebuild life for her three children
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — With her husband gone and three young children depending on her, Nor Nadia Abdul Majid, 32, is returning to her Perak hometown to rebuild her life — weeks after a driver, allegedly high on alcohol and drugs, killed Amirul Hafiz Omar, 32, on March 29. Nor Nadia, also 32, said her immediate focus is to remain strong for her three children — Muhammad Aqif Amsyar, 9, Muhammad Afif Izz Rayqal, 7, and Nur Humaira, 2 — who are still struggling to comprehend their father’s death. She said that she would be moving back to Kampung Repoh in Batu Kurau to be closer to her family for support, the New Straits Times reported.

items.title: Penang police dismantle drug lab, seize RM3.43m worth of narcotics in raids
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 2 — Penang police successfully dismantled a drug processing laboratory and busted a drug trafficking syndicate in a series of raids across the Timur Laut district on Monday and Tuesday, seizing drugs estimated to be worth RM3.43 million. State police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the integrated operation by the Penang Contingent Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department, carried out from 4pm on Monday until 9am Tuesday, led to the arrest of three individuals, including a foreign woman, and the seizure of various types of drugs. He said operation was carried out following intelligence gathering, which uncovered a house being used as a drug processing laboratory for methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA).

items.title: Petronas works with authorities to secure domestic supply, accelerates crude output drive
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) is working with the relevant authorities to secure domestic supply while accelerating efforts to raise crude production. Vice president of upstream development Mohd Nazlee Rasol said Petronas, as the national oil company, is ensuring supply security remains a priority, with ongoing engagements focused on identifying the fastest ways to increase output. Mohd Nazlee said Petronas has formed a task force to assess measures to boost production, adding that additional production would help ensure continued supply to its refineries.

items.title: Army corporal arrested after seven soldiers assaulted at Bentong camp; one in ICU
items.summary: BENTONG, April 2 — A 37-year-old army corporal has been arrested after allegedly assaulting seven younger soldiers at the 25th Battalion Royal Malay Regiment (RAMD) camp here, leaving one of the victims unconscious and in critical condition. Bentong district police chief Supt Zaiham Mohd Kahar confirmed the incident, saying a report was lodged on Tuesday morning by the 49-year-old father of one of the victims. “One of the victims, aged 24, was reported to have lost consciousness and is still receiving treatment at the Intensive Care Unit (ICU) of Hospital Sultan Haji Ahmad Shah, Temerloh,” Zaiham said in a statement last night.

items.title: TNB denies viral claims of April electricity tariff hike, lodges complaint with MCMC over rumours
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) has denied viral claims of an electricity tariff hike this month, calling them inaccurate and misleading. In a Facebook post, TNB clarified that any adjustments to electricity costs are governed by the Automatic Fuel Adjustment (AFA) mechanism. According to the company, the AFA mechanism, introduced in July last year, adjusts electricity costs according to global fuel prices and foreign exchange rates, with monthly adjustments verified by the Energy Commission (ST).

items.title: We cannot hold: Truckers warn of rising costs, halted projects as diesel surge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Malaysia’s lorry industry is taking a direct hit from surging diesel prices, with operators warning of halted construction, shrinking fleets and mounting pressure that could soon spill into higher costs across the economy. Malaysian Association of Land Lorry Drivers adviser Hairi Ali said the impact is being felt immediately on the ground, particularly among smaller operators. It will still go up,” he said, adding that operators are left with little choice.

items.title: Anwar: Petronas to help govt guarantee steady oil and gas supply until May
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Petroliam Nasional Bhd’s (Petronas) planning is assisting the government in providing sufficient oil and gas supplies at least until May, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. “This is very helpful to the people and the country,” he said. “The government would introduce austerity measures and a much more moderate celebration,” he said.

items.title: Anwar calls for crackdown on fake news over fuel and electricity prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The government has urged authorities, including the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), to take firm action against the growing spread of fake news on fuel and electricity prices. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said that, so far, the MCMC has identified 96 pieces of false content on social media related to the issue. So far, the MCMC has identified 96 pieces of fake content on fuel and electricity prices on social media,” he said.

items.title: Long road ahead from West Asia conflict, Malaysia remains resilient, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The prolonged conflict in West Asia is expected to require a lengthy recovery period, but Malaysia remains in a relatively stable economic position to weather the challenges, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said the situation has disrupted global supply chains, particularly in energy, although Malaysia has thus far managed to remain resilient. “When this happens, it will certainly affect global supply chains, especially energy, and Malaysia is not spared.

items.title: Anwar announces WFH for public sector and GLCs from April 15 to ease energy strain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The government today announced the implementation of work-from-home (WFH) arrangements for the public sector and government-linked companies (GLCs) effective April 15 as a proactive measure to address the global energy crisis. In a video message, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the decision, reached at the Cabinet meeting, aims to reduce fuel consumption and ensure the sustainability of the country’s energy supply, which has been affected by the conflict in West Asia. The prime minister also said that the government is intensifying efforts to diversify energy sources to safeguard the country’s energy supply.

items.title: Law minister confirms re-tabling of AG separation, PM term limit bill
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said following the implementation of Phase I of the Madani Accountability Framework in early 2026, the legal and institutional reform agenda is now moving into Phase II, spanning May to August. “(Also), entering Phase II beginning May, among the key initiatives to be implemented include the drafting of the Malaysian Ombudsman Bill and the Freedom of Information (FOI) Act at the next Parliamentary sitting,” she said in a post on Facebook. Azalina said among the institutional reform initiatives scheduled for implementation in April is the establishment of the Anti-Bullying Tribunal.

items.title: Who’s counting? MACC detains man after racking up RM4m in alleged fraudulent claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has detained an accounting service provider of a company for allegedly submitting documents containing false information related to travel claims worth RM4 million. Sources said the man, in his 30s, was detained yesterday while giving a statement at MACC headquarters in Putrajaya. The sources said preliminary investigations indicate the suspect allegedly committed the acts between 2023 and 2025.

items.title: Finance Ministry: Fuel subsidies maintained but BUDI95 quota reduced to 200 litres, as unsubsidised diesel breaches RM6 mark
items.summary: More critically, prices for refined products have soared, with petrol hitting US$150 per barrel and diesel reaching US$250 per barrel, placing significant upward pressure on retail prices. The other confirmed subsidised prices: Diesel (Sabah, Sarawak, and W.P. Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS): RM2.15 per litre.
